interesting: It was fun to see other peoples' memes.
useful: It helped put an article into a picture
costs: It kind of took away from my learning experience because I was too focused on making the memes and it was difficult finding articles each week. Maybe if we had to make one that might be fun and interesting, but every week it was a little stressful.

interesting: I really enjoyed the humor in it and I also enjoyed that it encouraged discussion and student/Instructor involvement in the class.
useful: The meme project allowed for several opportunities to learn how to navigate psychology journal articles, how to cite them properly, and how to find important information in those articles. It was also useful because the final project allowed for students to evaluate others and get solid opinions which encouraged the maker of the meme to dig deeper and learn how to correct mistakes. It was a very positive experience and the cause was that this was my most successful course my entire time in college. Everything I learned stuck much better than other courses because we talked about topics multiple times, and the schedule of all the posts and deadlines were very conducive for great communication but also in helping me stay on top of things despite struggling with ADHD.
costs: I do not have anything negative to say about this project.

interesting: I enjoyed finding images to go along with the articles I chose for each week.
useful: I had never considered creating my own memes before.  It is possible I will do this in the future for my job.
costs: I rarely received feedback from my peers about my memes, and I was somewhat frustrated with my peers using the same images over and over again each week.

interesting: I found it enjoyable because I got a chance to make them better than the first time around
useful: It helped me relate difficult things to understand into something easy
costs: Making a summary of an entire article

interesting: I liked that it allowed every student to put their own unique spin on the same pool of myths
useful: I summarized a long article into a few words or phrases
costs: It wasn't the best way to show that we as students understood the material

interesting: I liked that you could bring in your own creativity into this project since it was so open ended.
useful: It was useful for me to learn and understand the misconceptions and remember them in a way that is fun and appeals to pop culture.
costs: Some of the claims were just more difficult to find a meme that fit.

interesting: It was a way to include modern topics into school which was pretty cool
useful: It was a new way to display information I have learned
costs: No I don't think so

interesting: The fact that I look at memes daily that make me laugh, but this time I get to make one that makes people laugh.
useful: It was an interesting way to incorporate knowledge into something that will also make you laugh.
costs: I didn‚Äôt have any downsides.

interesting: I really like reading scientific articles about psychology. Using memes as a platform to summarize an article is an ingenious way of explaining a more technical paper to a large audience.
useful: I think it was useful because it allowed me to choose a meme that I wanted and use it as a PSA that a lot of people would understand.
costs: I didn't really see a down side to it. Revisions were difficult because it was hard to say "your meme sucks" or something of that nature. I think just one draft is fine for them personally.

interesting: I thought it was a helpful way to connect to the material that we were reading. It was something I had never done for a class before, and I really appreciate the different approach that the professor took to engagement.
useful: I don't necessarily see the meme project as something that is useful for me as I already know how to critically read articles. I also previously learned how to find empirically-based articles. However, as a doctoral student who is wanting to teach undergrads, I see how this project would be extremely beneficial in the learning and engagement process.
costs: I felt fairly embarrassed by some of my memes because I was not familiar with them. It is sometimes difficult for me to be creative, humorous, and informative and that felt consuming for me. At times, it felt like busy-work because I had already learned how to write a summary. However, I do see the benefits of this project.

interesting: I really enjoyed seeing the other creations and how creative some of the other students could be. I did enjoy looking at and commenting on other students work. I also liked creating my own - taking an idea and trying to convey that message in a meme. I found it interesting that I would try to present something in the meme, but not quite get it right, but a classmate or the instructor would say try this or make this adjustment (something obvious usually) then it would be a much better product (but I could not see the forest for the trees)!
useful: At first I was not excited about the meme project (had never created one or view them ) so I was unsure about it. After trying a few, it became a lot of fun and education in the studies associated with the memes were also great sources of information and discussion.
costs: I was stressed as never having done anything with memes and do not consider myself very creative but I found it very enjoyable. I think I made some very good memes and am proud of my work. There were a wide range of topics and meme styles, which I think is great, as long as that is communicated I would recommend continuing with this project.

interesting: Learning how to make a meme as I have never done this before.
useful: I may use memes for an online platform/business that I plane to create in the future.
costs: Time consuming

interesting: I liked being able to do something more creative to demonstrate my learning.
useful: It was useful for me because as an instructor, I have another way to keep my students engaged and entertained (memes are funny!).
costs: At times, I struggled to find an appropriate picture to use for my memes. Therefore, it was more time-consuming than I would have liked.

interesting: It was a medium I already knew about so it was fun to play around with it.
useful: I applied what I was learning to a comedic medium.
costs: There were lots of formatting hoops to jump through. Not with the meme itself, but rather with the explanatory paragraph with sources to explain the concept. It felt like too much work for a meme, which is supposed to be simple and self-explanatory.

interesting: Searching for the right picture to go with the saying.
useful: Summarizing information in a sarcastic, funny way.
costs: I hated having to explain the meme. I felt like the purpose of the meme was self-explanatory.

interesting: I'd never made memes before but I love sharing ones I find online, so making memes was a very fun challenge because I was able to use templates from some of my favorite memes (especially since the ones I used aren't exactly "trending" anymore!)
useful: I thought it was a good way to think like the younger generations do and communicate on their level.  I think this is a useful way to attempt countering the age of misinformation that we're in now.
costs: The main downside for me is that I'm not a very creative person so it took me a while to think of ideas for my memes.

interesting: The creativity of other people.
useful: Also by using the templates we see almost everyday, I think it will be easier to remember some of the information presented as we will associate that information to the template used.
costs: Initially, I thought it was easier to come up with memes that supported the myth rather than debunk it. This could be attributed to the fact that I have heard many of the myths throughout my life and/or the fact that there is more creativity allowed when not restricted to the findings of contradictory literature.

interesting: I am more creative for a class assignment.
useful: Somewhat valuable because I know now I can use memes for educational purposes as well, or as a way to better explain myself.
costs: It can be challenging to relate science-type literature to fit a meme correctly for it to be funny. Memes tend to be more enjoyable when it is related to pop culture.

interesting: Being able to see what classmates would choose as their foundation for the meme and how they debunked specific myths using it.
useful: Memes are meant to be funny in a sense, using it to challenge incorrect information could be an enjoyable teaching experience for everyone
costs: Because memes are generally funny, using it to debunk a myth could be seen as boring and some people may be uninterested in reading or understanding what the point of the meme was.

interesting: It was interesting seeing other peoples interpretations of the same topics. It seemed obvious which people had knowledge of meme culture beforehand and who did not get it.
useful: It helped for relating scientific knowledge with today's social media. It makes that knowledge easier to understand for the youths.
costs: Some topics were harder to make a successful meme out of. Memes are simple with little information, and some topics cannot be condensed down in that state; at least not well.

interesting: It was different, made me think outside the box, and made me research and apply scientific knowledge in a different way that I will not easily forget.
useful: Forced me to learn in a different way.  And I learned how to create a meme.
costs: Initial apprehension and some anxiety since I had not created a meme before.

interesting: It was something I had never done in a class before. It put a spin on a project that made it more interesting and appealing.
useful: I definitely learned a lot about neuromyths! This can help me in my practice.
costs: Nothing really. I was a little stressed out at first when thinking about doing this project - it was out of my comfort zone and like nothing I had ever done before. But once I got started, it was actually quite fun.

interesting: It was interesting trying to convey a myth and the truth all in just a few words and finding a picture to help convey the message too.
useful: It challenged me to think about consolidating practical information related to psychology in a way that will make sense and be relatable to those not studying psychology.
costs: No real downsides. I don't use social media much so it felt a little like I won't be able to actually use this, but I have shown the meme to people in conversation.

interesting: I got to make memes for school.
useful: It was useful for me to concisely relay my knowledge into a format that was easy to follow and understand.
costs: Memes are an inside joke on the entire internet. Trying to make a widely known inside joke that's niche can be difficult to convey.

interesting: the many ways I could interpret the background picture
useful: I did learn something and I retained that knowledge when I found the meme especially funny
costs: being stuck on choosing the best caption

interesting: I enjoyed sharing my humor with the class while also explaining my thoughts about debunking a myth about child development.
useful: I think the meme project gave students a chance to express their thoughts by showing their humor and explain what their meme was about. This also gives student a chance to show some creativity instead of writing a paper, the professor gets a chance to know her students better and enjoy grading the assignment.
costs: I've seen memes before but I'm not huge into them, so the project would be hard if a person had never heard of memes or weren't really into them.

interesting: I think it made me get a more in depth view of the topics i chose since it had me read the information, summarize it in my own words, and understand it enough to make a meme out of it.
useful: I think yes because I learned information on the topics I chose and it will help since I want to be an elementary education teacher and also a parent.
costs: It was hard to figure out how to create the meme, especially since I don't normally use memes in my everyday life, so I had to look up what a meme was and how to do it because I didn't know much about memes in general

interesting: i could put out my creatatvity
useful: it gave me more experience for making memes
costs: instructions where a little confusing

interesting: I enjoyed looking at the meme templates and looking at my classmates memes.
useful: I found it useful to add humor to scientific data. Using memes is a much less dense way to remember the claims and how they were debunked. Using memes has also helped me to remember the information the memes are about.
costs: I found it difficult when making the memes to be sure I debunked the claim. Sometimes I felt my class and I would make the meme to go along with the claim instead of debunk the claim.

interesting: I enjoyed thinking about how the pictures and statement can relate to each other. The opportunity challenged me to think something outside of the box.
useful: It was useful and easy to share opinions about the statements by using meme.
costs: I would like to have more clear explanation about the project if that's possible.

interesting: I thought it was interesting and funny. I enjoyed learning about different myths and seeing others memes.
useful: It will be useful for me in the future if I have children and knowing what is true and what isn't true.
costs: I had a hard time coming up with what people needed to work on for there memes, because most of the ones I read were really good.

interesting: I thought getting to pick on and come up with your own sayings was fun. I did a clown one that was very funny.
useful: It helped me better understand misconceptions that will also help me in the future.
costs: Explaining my meme was not too hard but also was not fun but I understand why we did it.

interesting: being able to make memes on whatever you wanted
useful: yes I will be able to make more memes now
costs: Trying to think of what meme to use

interesting: It was really cool that we got to turn our findings into something that everyone in our age groups relates to/understands.
useful: It was very useful because it taught me how to take something very descriptive and scientific into something fun, simple, and understandable.
costs: I was scared that my memes weren‚Äôt funny enough.

interesting: I really enjoyed looking at my peers memes as well.
useful: It was useful for me now because I'm hoping o get points for it.
costs: Trying to remember good meme I've seen and how they were set up.

interesting: Learning how to make memes and how to use them to help enlighten others on the issues displayed.
useful: I now know how to make memes to impress/annoy my friends. But seriously, it increased my knowledge on issues I was mostly uninformed of.
costs: I found it to be easy and very enjoyable. I didn't feel any downside.

interesting: I liked that we got to create our memes and have the choice to be creative.
useful: I learned a lot from reading and making my own memes.
costs: I do not think there was a downside.

interesting: It was very different from anything I have done in any other class this year, which I really enjoyed.
useful: Not only doing research on my own myths that I chose, but reading other students memes helps me gain even more knowledge that I can use when I myself become a parent some day.
costs: I can't think of any downsides

interesting: I like that we had a lot of options of topics we could choose from. I like getting to read about them and pick the ones I liked
useful: The information I learned will stay with me better than had I just read it from a textbook, so it will be more helpful with recall later in life.
costs: One downside was that many people did some of the same topics, but even with this there was a variation in take always so it wasn‚Äôt really a downside.

interesting: it was nice to have a simple project where you were able to learn about common myths and misconceptions.
useful: In the future, if I can make memes for students about stuff we are learning
costs: I have no downsides to this project

interesting: I liked coming up with my own meme and then looking at everyone else's. I got a good laugh.
useful: I learned about myths that a lot of parents think are true and I never knew that a lot of people actually thought those things.
costs: Honestly I do not think there was any.

interesting: It was fun to make the memes I see everyday my own.
useful: I learned more about the course material, which is useful information I will use when I have kids of my own, and I learned more about how to use the popular internet.
costs: I don't have any downsides because I enjoyed the project.

interesting: Learning about a common misconception about child development and then proving it true or false with a meme was interesting.
useful: Yes, the meme project was useful to me as it helped me comprehend the truths about child development and helped my analyisis skills.
costs: There were not any downsides of the meme project in my eyes.

interesting: It was super funny to see all the memes that the class created. Also made understanding the myths very easy.
useful: It made learning the truth of the myths very easy and fast. Some of which will help once becoming a parent.
costs: Not really sure about any downsides. Only thing I didn't like was the fact that we had to do both drafts and final memes, I don't think people made many changes.

interesting: The meme project was enjoyable because technology is so popular and I use social media a lot so it was fun to do something that relates to me and my peers. It was also funny while still being educational. I thought it was interesting to read about some myths that I thought were true about child development and learn that they actually were not. I liked how we were able to choose our own myth from a wide variety, that way we could choose a topic we were interested in. I think it makes learning a lot easier when I am reading and writing about something I find engaging.
useful: This project was useful because I now know certain misconceptions about kids that will be beneficial once I become a become a teacher and interact with my students or when I raise children of my own.
costs: One downside was the peer review. I find that with a lot of assignments that require peer review, like in my English class as well, I always try to give in depth feedback to others so that I can actually help them and they can utilize my suggestions, but when I look back at the feedback I get, it is usually very vague and pointless. That is a little frustrating because the purpose of the draft and peer review was so that we could get different perspectives and make changes before turning in our final draft, but I couldn't find many beneficial pieces of advice from my classmates.

interesting: Since the memes were relevant to my major, they were especially funny. I enjoyed looking through the discussion boards laughing and just seeing other people's takes on something.
useful: The meme project was useful because it debunked common myths, some of which people very strongly believe. I also got to know the background behind these misconceptions. It is important for me to know why these myths are inaccurate for my future career.
costs: The downside of the meme project is that we didn't go very in depth into the course content. Since we didn't cover these misconceptions in class, we exclusively learned about them on our own through the short articles.

interesting: It was fun to read other peoples memes while also learning myths
useful: It debunked a lot of claims I thought were true
costs: It was a little time consuming

interesting: It was interesting to see the other memes from my classmates and how they interpreted everything.
useful: It made me really understand the content
costs: It was a bit time consuming and I wasn't sure if my meme was good enough

interesting: I enjoyed reading new articles each week.
useful: It was useful because I had to show my work to others. It made me try hard and boosted my class confidence.
costs: The downsides were doing it every week. I think it would be good to do every other week.

interesting: I was interested in looking up scientific articles and reading about the experiments the researchers did. I think this is because I will be participating in research next semester.
useful: The knowledge about educational psychology is useful both now and in the future; however, I will probably not remember the information because of the meme.
costs: It was sometimes difficult to find a primary research article about a topic we discussed that week, but still very possible.

interesting: I liked seeing other classmates interpretations of what they learned about the materials, and how they applied it to be "funny" in a meme format.
useful: I thought that it was more useful to be able to apply the lessons learned in class to the project, and finding useful and relatable articles was a good way to prompt research in the field of educational psychology.
costs: It was overwhelming for me with so many different choices of options in what to do the project on each week, I had a hard time finding articles that were relevant to the same topics of what we covered in class.

interesting: Creating meme's. I have never created a meme before. So it was fun once I got past the initial, Oh crap I have no idea what I am doing. :)
useful: It got me out of my comfort zone. I had to critique others work when I was unsure of my own.  Overall I feel like it was a good experience that really did help me.
costs: I would not say downside but it was a little stressful trying to find the right article, creating the meme and waiting for the students critiques. However they were never bad critiques, they were useful.

interesting: It‚Äôs was nice to not do a traditional writing assignment
useful: I think it helps seek out other useful resources about topics covered
costs: No real downsides

interesting: It was actually fun to make the memes and I always laughed at them
useful: Helped cement the idea learned in each journal I read
costs: Nothing

interesting: The thing I liked most was being able to practice searching for and reading scientific journals / empirical journals.
useful: It was useful that videos were provided to show how to find the journals and how to do everything else, step by step.
costs: It was a little more time consuming than I would've liked but it was worth it!

interesting: It was fun to apply the lens of scientific analysis to fairly silly memes. The process also allowed me to explore and share the emotions that I experienced while reading several articles, which is something one doesn't often get to do.
useful: I could see myself using the memes I made in classes that I teach, or using the same technique to make new memes.
costs: It required quite a bit of time and I'm not sure there was a lot of return on investment. It might have been valuable once or twice, but by the time the fourth meme came around I don't think I was learning anything new aside from what I read in the articles.

interesting: I liked seeing everyone else's memes and how they summarized the information from the claims they were meme-ing about. I also liked reading through all the claims prior to picking out the ones I would make memes about- the articles were very interesting.
useful: I learned some interesting things from the articles. Since the articles were so short they couldn't go into much detail which left me curious about the topics they discussed.
costs: I was a little confused as to why we were making memes. At first I had a difficult time seeing the point of the exercise. After thinking about it a little more, I understand that it's a good way to summarize what we had learned from the articles and see how we summarized them.

interesting: It was a nice break from the conventional write-about-an-article project, which I think most classes utilize too much. However, the research field focuses on a lot of reading research articles and processing the information.
useful: Again, it was a nice break from the routine assignments. Being a senior, I‚Äôve covered a lot of the information numerous times and I‚Äôm not really into memes, so I didn‚Äôt get a lot of knowledge out of it. However, I see how the short, cut-to-the chase articles are useful for students just starting out. I personally at this point in my studies need more in depth articles, but I don‚Äôt think the project in inappropriate for the class and typical student in any way.
costs: As I explained above, I like articles more in depth and linking the physical research to the evidence, but that is just where I am in my academic path. I feel most students in this class are typically freshman or sophomores.

interesting: N/A
useful: N/A
costs: N/A

interesting: Coming from a background of writing manuscripts and other 'less-laymans' writing it really helped me get back in touch with writing simply and shortly. It's so important to communicate well and in simple terms and I think this project allowed me to get back in touch with that.
useful: It allowed me to better communicate ideas in a more palatable form. It is better to write like Shel Silverstein instead of Hemmingway with scientific work.
costs: None.

interesting: Getting to educate others while having fun
useful: It helped to debunk some common myths and misconceptions
costs: There were no downsides

interesting: Picking out a format for the meme.
useful: Improved grammar/writing skills by having other classmates review my memes.
costs: There wasn't any downsides to the meme project. Overall, it was a fun experience.

interesting: What was interesting about this project is that it made us think outside the constructs of regular learning and lecture. Yes, we could have read about the articles and written about them, but this gave way to a more creative side of things. It also helped make us describe the article enough so that the meme would be understood.
useful: I will use them more in the future (I don't have a regular class) but I think they could be beneficial to a point.
costs: At first I was unsure about the project. I think now, having a class do this project, having some examples would be helpful. I didn't quite understand it at first until I saw other classmates and what they came up with.

interesting: When I finally understood what a meme was and the pattern used to create the meme.
useful: Gaining more knowledge of a societal phenomena.
costs: Not understanding what they were and how to make one. - frustration of the learning curve.

interesting: it was fun to be creative in graduate school! I enjoyed seeing other student's memes.
useful: It pushed me to make my research relatable, which is helpful in the future when I present or discuss my work with others.
costs: I am not the most creative person so there were times it felt out of my comfort zone. But overall, not bad!

interesting: I think it was interesting to see the creativity of other students and how they summarized their articles.
useful: I think in the age of technology it makes scientific articles more engaging and could be something to use with students in the future.
costs: I think it was kind of time consuming and as someone who is not very creative it was more difficult for me to think of a meme. Additionally it was hard to get the representation of a scientific article in one meme.

interesting: I definitely thought the meme project was a more unique and interesting way of engaging with the articles we read for class. I would MUCH rather do a meme than other sorts of writing assignments often assigned in classes.
useful: I learned to make memes, so that was kind of fun. A lot of the articles I read weren't particularly useful for me, but honestly I think that's a program design issue and not an issue of this class. If I was going into k12 education or counseling then I DO think this would have been useful for me.
costs: I do think between the readings, lecture, discussion posts, and meme - class was very time demanding. My memes took a while each week. To find the article, read it, come up with the meme, and write the summary/analysis too.

interesting: Using another type of learning that what is typically required.
useful: It provided a different way to look at and complete an assignment.  Being a teacher I am looking at having my students do something similar.
costs: Not sure I was doing it correctly.

interesting: I think it was interesting to use my creative juices and think of a meme in relation to the topic. I love memes and had fun doing the project
useful: I think its useful because it can help us simplify hard to understand concepts. If you can simplify a concept through a meme, then the general public can be aware of it and be more mindful about it
costs: I had a hard time thinking of a meme that would be entertaining and funny and also makes sense in terms of the topic. But all in all, I had fun doing it.

interesting: I had never had a project like this before. I thought it was an interesting twist on a project. At first I was very intimidated by the project and it took me a while to read through the instructions and get a grasp on how exactly to go about completing this project but once I got over that hill, it was enjoyable to be able to go through meme makers and be able to laugh while doing homework! I don‚Äôt think that is a combination we find often and I think that was really nice. It also made doing the research more enjoyable.
useful: Some of the cognitive neuromyths I was unaware of, so I think overall learning about the myths and then being intrigued to go read or learn more about them was the most useful part.
costs: I think the main confusion I had was just whether or not the myth was busted in the meme or not. Sometimes I found it hard to figure out whether I was debunking the myth or supporting the myth just with the nature of how memes are (sarcasm).

interesting: It was a different twist to normal graduate assignments. Once I understood the concept (that the meme itself had to dispel a rumor.. for whatever reason I just could not grasp that at first!), it was somewhat enjoyable to apply a humorous connotation to what are generally dry subject areas.
useful: I generally was not aware of a good amount of the myths on the list, so for education purposes I feel I learned a significant amount on different biases that circulate among the population.
costs: I don't necessarily feel that this project stressed me out or caused me excess anxiety, but to have the back and forth of posting to discussion boards, commenting on other students' posts, and having to submit a final copy kind of did, if that makes sense. I really generally dislike the ideation of critiquing others' work because I don't feel like I'm in a position to do so, so just having to do that weighs on me and requires extra time and thought to word things in a constructive yet non-offensive way. I probably would have rather written an additional paper instead of creating memes because they've never really been my thing, but that's just me.

interesting: I liked having to use a modern concept in class. It was interesting to create something that is seen almost every day by most people
useful: It made me dig deeper into information about the topic and also helped me to learn how to find sources on sites other than the library page
costs: The downside was editing the meme. Some of the formats were hard to type on and took some adjusting

interesting: Seeing the different memes from the rest of the class
useful: Memes are everywhere so it helps having a project that can be relatable
costs: Kind of have to be creative and not a lot of people enjoy that

interesting: I just enjoy making memes. It's a quick, easy, and fun way to create relatable content that puts things in perspective lots of people can understand.
useful: Let's me practice packaging information in a familiar, but newer format so it can be conveyed in a quick and fun manner.
costs: The biggest downside here is my wanting to use irony in a 'scientifically accurate meme' because irony isn't always super obvious, but memeing loves irony.

interesting: Finding information about the topic I chose and relaying it in a new format
useful: Finding key words to describe a particular topic
costs: N/A

interesting: I really enjoyed the creativity that was involved in the meme project. It made me think outside of the box. It also made me laugh while creating it.
useful: I think the meme project show me a creative way to relay information to people. It's a great learning opportunity.
costs: I can't think of any. I enjoyed this project.

interesting: It was a nice change from writing papers.
useful: Honestly not sure it is but it was interesting.
costs: Not sure there were downsides. Just not sure how valuable the skill is.

interesting: There really were no downsides.
useful: Female

interesting: I enjoyed researching the different neuromyths and figuring out entertaining ways to debunk them.
useful: I think the most beneficial aspect to this project was being forced to summarize findings in a limited number of words. This project required two parts - identifying a myth and debunking it. That is not easy to do with limited text, but I think this is a skill that translates to so many other parts of work or life. Most grad school papers are heavy on length and detail, but in the real world we need to make points quickly and succinctly (like with an elevator pitch) or risk losing someone's attention. This project forced us to do that.
costs: The only downside was that I personally felt silly telling friends or family that I had to make memes for grad school because it doesn't sound very serious or academic.

interesting: It was a fun way to incorporate class work to modern trends!
useful: It helped me analyze cognitive psychology from a different point of view. It made me think about the topics I chose differently.
costs: It was a bit challenging to fit all the reasons the myths were debunked in one paragraph.

interesting: Seeing what everyone came up with using all the myth options given to us and just seeing how creative people were
useful: now I know how to make memes if I ever decide to do so in the future
costs: I didn't understand how to put them meme together at first

interesting: I enjoyed reading about the specific articles and then trying to find just a few words from it to make a meme. It was challenging but fun!
useful: I think learning about the myths was very helpful for anyone but especially for me since I am going into the field of education.
costs: I found it a little confusing at first just because I had never created a meme prior to the meme project.

interesting: Seeing other student's creativity and just look at what other peers came up with because some people might have done the same topic but the memes were completely different.
useful: The meme project showed me that I am capable of making one and how to do one. This expanded my technology knowledge.
costs: The downside probably was giving critiques. Most rough draft memes were perfect but we had to give good and bad feedback. It was just hard to come up with some bad feedback.

interesting: I enjoyed finding and reading about specific proof about how to debunk the myths.
useful: It was great to have proof for some of my beliefs and to find scientific proof backing it.
costs: I find it hard to criticize other students, and it can sometimes be hard to be creative about these kinds of things.

interesting: It was interesting going through the myth list and choosing which ones we could do. It was also enjoyable that we could use whatever template we wanted.
useful: It was useful because it made me look at myths I did not know were myths instead of just focusing on the ones that I did for marks.
costs: The downsides were trying to find feedback to give to some people, some made really good meme and descriptions.

interesting: I enjoyed learning about all the misconceptions about child development and making a meme making fun of it that made me and my peers laugh. I also enjoyed reading other people's memes.
useful: It was useful because now I know many of the misconceptions surrounding child development and why they are untrue and am able to educate other people about them.
costs: The downside to the meme project was giving detailed feedback to others because many people's memes were very good and had little to fix.

interesting: It was a fun creative way to learn the material. I liked reading other memes too they were so good!
useful: It helped me learn more about child development and even myths that I believed to be true!
costs: I had trouble with formatting, but I troubleshooted and it was fine!

interesting: It was something different than I have done before in a class so it was super fun to do something different especially because I felt like it helped me understand the myths better!
useful: It helped me realize that there are so many different myths and that I will help me educate other people about these myths in the future.
costs: I have never made a meme previously so I was worried at first because I thought it would not be good but once I made my first meme it worked out well!

interesting: This was a project that I got excited for as soon as I heard about it. I've already dabbled in meme making a bit, so I was able to play around my project memes a bit, which made it fun. I felt it was a great way to incorporate class lessons in a fun manner.
useful: Learning about some of the myths of child development is important to my future career as a K-12 Music Teacher. Learning more about how children develop and some misconceptions behind their development will help me to better understand how to work with them.
costs: The only improvement I would make would be to find more myths to debunk. This would give the class more material to work with. Overall I felt this project was fairly well-structured as is.

interesting: I liked seeing other people‚Äôs memes and how creative they were. It was interesting to see other people‚Äôs ideas or perspective on a concept.
useful: It was useful because the myths are common thing that teachers and parents encounter so it is important to know the facts rather than myths.
costs: Some people may not be as creative so some may have struggle with this project.

interesting: I really enjoyed reading the articles.
useful: I enjoyed learning the information in the articles, I would have liked to read more of them because they had good information conveyed in a concise and effective way.
costs: I'm the kind of person who doesn't like creative projects, I would have learned better and felt less stressed if I had to read all the articles and write a quiz on them, or write a paper explaining what I had learned.

interesting: I learned a lot when I read about the various myths regarding child development.
useful: I am an elementary education major, so one day I might be a teacher and be surrounded by children. By knowing that many common beliefs regarding child development are not completely true, I will better be able to provide kids with what they need the most and avoid doing things for them that are ineffective.
costs: It is easy to explain why and how the common beliefs are misconceptions  with a written paragraph, but explaining this information through a meme was difficult because meme messages are short, so I could not use too much detail to explain what I wanted to say. I also had to find images that were relevant to my messages, which required some creative thinking. The memes are a cool idea and I did not dislike the assignment, but I think that it was probably more work than a writing assignment.

interesting: It was so much fun to do something other than writing a paper! Yes we had to write a couple paragraphs but it was so fun being able to turn our myths into our own funny memes.
useful: Gave me an opportunity to work on my meme making skills.
costs: N/A

interesting: Making memes and getting to see the what kind of sense of humor my peers have.
useful: Yes, it was fun and informative to read numerous articles and try to make a funny way of showing that the myths are false.
costs: N/A

interesting: I thought it was interesting because it was a different assignment than what we are used to. It brought a new level of creativity to the classroom, and it made discussion board much more enjoyable.
useful: I thought the meme project made me more engaged in my readings than simply writing a paper because you really have to analyze the entire article to sum it up in one single meme. I also thought it made me more engaged in other people's discussion boards which allowed me to gain more knowledge from my peers.
costs: I didn't think there were any downsides.

interesting: It allowed me to read and learn about different myths in child development. I also felt that the format to read about what other people chose was very enjoyable and unique.
useful: It allowed me to gain knowledge on the three topics I chose as I believe that as a future educator, that knowledge will be helpful in the future.
costs: None that I saw. I really liked having to do three different topics

interesting: it is something I've never done in any of my classes but in order to create a meme that made sense you had to understand what the main idea was which helped me understand the material presented
useful: I now have a better idea of myths dealing with child development.
costs: I honestly didn't think there was any downsides, I would much rather do a meme over a paper.

interesting: Learning about the different myths and then being able to create something out of it.
useful: It taught me that certain things that I had heard about child development before this class is not true.
costs: No, I really enjoyed it.

interesting: I found this project enjoyable because it was different than doing a boring reading assignment and it also made you understand what you were reading to be able to make a meme about it.
useful: I found this project useful because there are many myths about children and for me majoring in elementary education it can be useful to know.
costs: I did not find any downsides I really liked this project it was a way to learn about myths in a way that made you really understand the topics and it was not boring.

interesting: I liked being able to look at other memes made by classmates and being able to reply to them and critique them.
useful: Some of these myths i thought we‚Äôre true at a certain time so it‚Äôs good to know that these are all myths.
costs: The downside was trying to come up with a meme for me. It took me a while to come up with a funny meme for my myths.

interesting: I enjoyed reading the articles the most!
useful: It was useful to know the myths and why they were wrong.
costs: For me, the hardest part was trying to come up with what to say on the memes to try and make it funny.
